title: Find your starting point
intro: A short Italian check that gets harder as it goes, so we can start you in the right place. It takes about two minutes and you can retake it any time.

questions.prompt: 'Io ___ italiano.' (I am Italian)
questions.explanation: With io the verb essere is sono.

questions.prompt: Which form is correct for 'the student' (a male student)?
questions.explanation: A masculine noun starting with s plus a consonant takes the article lo.

questions.prompt: 'Noi ___ due gatti.' (we have two cats)
questions.explanation: The noi form of avere is abbiamo.

questions.prompt: 'Loro ___ in centro.' (abitare, they live)
questions.explanation: Regular are verbs take ano in the loro form.

questions.prompt: Which form is correct for 'a friend' when the friend is female?
questions.explanation: Feminine una drops its vowel before another vowel and takes an apostrophe.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says 'I do not speak Spanish'?
questions.explanation: Negation uses non placed directly before the verb.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of 'la città'?
questions.explanation: Nouns ending in a stressed vowel do not change in the plural, only the article does.

questions.prompt: 'Le ragazze sono ___.' (tall)
questions.explanation: The adjective agrees with a feminine plural noun, so it ends in e.

questions.prompt: '___ sorella si chiama Anna.' (my sister)
questions.explanation: Singular family members drop the article before a possessive.

questions.prompt: 'I libri sono ___ zaino.' (in the backpack)
questions.explanation: Zaino takes the article lo, and in plus lo contracts to nello.

questions.prompt: '___ i film italiani.' (I like Italian films)
questions.explanation: Piacere agrees with the thing liked, and i film is plural.

questions.prompt: 'Maria è ___ a Roma ieri.' (andare)
questions.explanation: With the auxiliary essere the participle agrees with the subject, here feminine singular.

questions.prompt: 'Mentre ___ , è suonato il telefono.' (I was studying)
questions.explanation: An action in progress that is interrupted takes the imperfetto.

questions.prompt: 'Ieri Marco ___ tornato tardi dal lavoro.'
questions.explanation: Tornare is a verb of movement and builds the passato prossimo with essere.

questions.prompt: 'Quando ___ piccoli, abitavamo a Napoli.' (we were)
questions.explanation: A lasting state in the past uses the imperfetto of essere, eravamo.

questions.prompt: 'Hai comprato le mele? Sì, le ho ___.'
questions.explanation: After the direct object pronoun le the participle agrees as feminine plural.

questions.prompt: '___ ho telefonato ieri sera.' (I phoned him)
questions.explanation: Telefonare takes an indirect object, and the masculine indirect pronoun is gli.

questions.prompt: 'Marco mi presta la bici. Marco ___ presta.' (lends it to me)
questions.explanation: Before a direct object pronoun mi becomes me, and bici is feminine so it takes la.

questions.prompt: 'Quanti fratelli hai? ___ ho due.'
questions.explanation: Ne replaces a quantity expression and means of them.

questions.prompt: 'Vai spesso a Milano? Sì, ___ vado ogni mese.'
questions.explanation: Ci stands for a place already mentioned, here a Milano.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct informal command telling a friend not to worry?
questions.explanation: A negative tu command is non plus the infinitive, with the reflexive pronoun before or attached.

questions.prompt: What is the futuro semplice of essere for loro?
questions.explanation: Essere has the irregular future stem sar, giving saranno.

questions.prompt: '___ un caffè, per favore.' (I would like)
questions.explanation: The condizionale presente vorrei is the polite way to say I would like.

questions.prompt: 'Questo esercizio è più facile ___ quello.'
questions.explanation: Comparing two nouns or pronouns uses di, while che compares two qualities or actions.

questions.prompt: 'In Italia ___ molto caffè.' (people drink a lot of coffee)
questions.explanation: The si impersonale takes the third person singular with a singular noun.

questions.prompt: 'Sto ___ una lettera.' (I am writing)
questions.explanation: Stare plus the gerundio describes an action happening right now.

questions.prompt: 'Quando sono arrivato, il treno ___ già partito.'
questions.explanation: An event finished before another past event takes the trapassato prossimo with era.

questions.prompt: 'Penso che lui ___ ragione.' (avere)
questions.explanation: Verbs of opinion such as pensare take the congiuntivo presente in the subordinate clause.

questions.prompt: 'Spero ___ presto.' (I hope to arrive soon)
questions.explanation: When both verbs share the same subject, sperare takes di plus the infinitive.

questions.prompt: '___ sia stanco, esco lo stesso.' (although)
questions.explanation: Benché governs the congiuntivo, while anche se takes the indicativo.

questions.prompt: 'Se ___ più tempo, viaggerei di più.'
questions.explanation: The second periodo ipotetico pairs the congiuntivo imperfetto with the condizionale presente.

questions.prompt: 'Credevo che lui ___ già partito.'
questions.explanation: A past main verb pulls the subordinate clause into the congiuntivo trapassato, fosse partito.

questions.prompt: 'Sono contento che tu ___ superato l'esame.'
questions.explanation: A present main verb with a completed action takes the congiuntivo passato, abbia superato.

questions.prompt: 'Se lo ___ , te lo avrei detto.' (if I had known)
questions.explanation: The third periodo ipotetico uses the congiuntivo trapassato with the condizionale passato.

questions.prompt: 'Mi ha detto che ___ più tardi.' (that he would arrive later)
questions.explanation: Italian expresses the future seen from a past moment with the condizionale passato.

questions.prompt: 'Quando ___ finito i compiti, potrai uscire.'
questions.explanation: An action completed before another future action takes the futuro anteriore.

questions.prompt: 'Il libro ___ ti ho parlato è in biblioteca.'
questions.explanation: Parlare takes di, so the relative pronoun keeps the preposition and becomes di cui.

questions.prompt: 'La porta ___ chiusa ogni sera alle otto.' (gets closed)
questions.explanation: Venire builds a passive that stresses the action, while andare would mean must be closed.

questions.prompt: 'In quel negozio ___ scarpe italiane.' (Italian shoes are sold)
questions.explanation: In the si passivante the verb agrees with the plural subject scarpe.
